objectiveEn: About one hour. Learn 20 feeling words, adjectives + 'because', listening, speaking, homework.
objectiveMn: Ойролцоогоор 1 цаг. 20 мэдрэмжийн үг, тэмдэг нэр + 'because' дүрэм, сонсох, ярих, гэрийн даалгавар.
contentEn: Express feelings with 'I am / I feel + adjective' and explain reasons with 'because'.
contentMn: Мэдрэмжээ 'I am / I feel + тэмдэг нэр' илэрхийлж, шалтгааныг 'because'-ээр тайлбарлана.

vocabulary.example: I am happy today!
She is sad because she lost.
Don't be angry with me!
I am scared of big dogs.
She is excited about the trip.
He is bored at home today.
I am so tired after school.
She is stressed about the exam.
He is surprised by the gift.
I am confused by this question.
She is proud of her good result.
I am worried about my exam.
He feels lonely without friends.
I am nervous before the exam.
She is very calm and relaxed.
I am fine, thank you.
I feel great today!
I feel terrible today.
How do you feel today?
I am happy because it is Friday.

quiz.question: I am happy ___ today is Friday.
Why is Bat nervous?
quiz.explanation: Bat said he has an exam today.

quiz.question: How does Sarnai feel?
quiz.explanation: Sarnai feels calm because she studied a lot.

quiz.question: How does Bat feel after the exam?
quiz.explanation: Bat said he feels so happy and relieved.

quiz.question: How does the teacher feel?
quiz.explanation: The teacher said she is happy and proud of both.

quiz.question: How do you ask about someone's feeling?
quiz.explanation: Correct question form: 'How do you feel?'

page1.grammarTopic: How do you feel? — Adjectives for feelings + because
page1.grammarExplanation: To talk about feelings, use 'I am + adjective' or 'I feel + adjective'. Ask: 'How do you feel?'. Explain reasons with 'because': 'I am happy because today is Friday.' You can also use Present Continuous: 'I am feeling better now.' Opposites: happy↔sad, excited↔bored, calm↔nervous. Мэдрэмжийн тухай: 'I am + тэмдэг нэр' эсвэл 'I feel + тэмдэг нэр'. Шалтгааныг 'because'-ээр илэрхийлнэ.
quickPractice: 2. He is _____ because it is Monday. (sad)
4. He is _____ at home with nothing to do. (bored)
commonMistakes: I am feel happy. → I am happy. / I feel happy.
She is tiredly. → She is tired.
I feel boredly. → I feel bored.
Why you are sad? → Why ARE you sad?

page2.readingPassage: Today is exam day. Bat is very nervous because the exam is very important. His friend Sarnai is calm because she studied hard. After the exam, Bat is happy and excited because he thinks he did well. Sarnai is proud of herself. Their teacher is happy because all the students tried their best. What a great day!

keyPhrases.mongolian: Би одоо илүү дээр мэдэрч байна

speakingQuestions: 1. How do you feel today? / Та өнөөдөр яаж мэдэрч байна вэ?
2. What makes you happy? / Та юунаас баяртай болдог вэ?
5. How do you feel before an exam?
6. How do you feel when you speak English?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I feel a little nervous actually.

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Because I have an English test today!

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Don't worry! You are very good at English.

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Thank you. That makes me feel better.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: How do you feel after the test?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I feel happy and relieved! It was not so bad.

page2.speakingTip: Don't worry about mistakes — just keep talking! Every mistake helps you learn faster. Confidence is more important than perfection at this stage.

page3.listeningScript: Teacher: Good morning! How does everyone feel today? Bat: I feel nervous, teacher. We have an exam today! Teacher: That's natural! Sarnai, how do you feel? Sarnai: I feel calm because I studied a lot last night. Teacher: Excellent! After the exam — Bat, how do you feel now? Bat: I feel so happy and relieved! I think I did well! Sarnai: I feel proud of myself! I answered all the questions. Teacher: I am very happy and proud of both of you! Well done!

listeningQuestions.question: How does Bat feel at the start?
How does Bat feel after the exam?
How does Sarnai feel after the exam?
What word connects feeling and reason?

grammarPractice: 2. He is _____ because it is Monday. (sad)
homework: Writing: Write 5 sentences about how you feel today and WHY (use 'because').
Vocabulary: Draw 5 emoji faces and write the English feeling word under each.
Speaking: Ask 3 people 'How do you feel today?' — write their answers.
Review: Learn the opposite pairs: happy↔sad · excited↔bored · calm↔nervous.
completionSummary: Listened to a classroom feelings dialog
